Let her know she's safe with you and that she didn't do anything wrong. Try to ask gentle, open questions like ŌĆśCan you tell me more about the games?' without suggesting answers or pressuring her. Don't promise to keep secrets if something isn't safe, you'll need to get help to protect her.
It's important to involve professionals as soon as possible this could mean contacting a child protection service, a pediatrician, or a child psychologist who is trained to handle situations like this. They can guide you on how to proceed and help make sure your daughter is safe. If you believe there's any immediate risk, don't wait contact local authorities right away.
You're doing the right thing by taking this seriously and looking for help.
